title: 11.1 GENERAL

data.rule: 11.1 GENERAL

title: 11.1.1 Purpose

data.rule: 11.1.1 Purpose

title: Chapter 11 presents criteria for the design and construction of buildings and other structures subject to earthquake ground motions. The speci fi ed earthquake loads are based on postelastic energy di

data.rule: Chapter 11 presents criteria for the design and construction of buildings and other structures subject to earthquake ground motions. The speci fi ed earthquake loads are based on postelastic energy dissipation in the structure. Because of this fact, the requirements for design, detailing, and construction shall be satis fi ed, even for structures and members for which load combinations that do not include earthquake loads indicate larger demands than combinations that include earthquake loads.

title: CHAPTER 11

data.rule: CHAPTER 11 SEISMIC DESIGN CRITERIA

title: Buildings whose purpose is to enclose equipment or machinery and whose occupants are engaged in maintenance or monitoring of that equipment, machinery, or their associated processes shall be permitted to be classi fi ed as nonbuilding structures designed and detailed in accordance with Section 15.5 of this standard.

data.rule: Buildings whose purpose is to enclose equipment or machinery and whose occupants are engaged in maintenance or monitoring of that equipment, machinery, or their associated processes shall be permitted to be classi fi ed as nonbuilding structures designed and detailed in accordance with Section 15.5 of this standard.

title: 11.1.2 Scope

data.rule: 11.1.2 Scope

title: Every structure and portion thereof, including nonstructural components, shall be designed and constructed to resist the effects of earthquake motions as prescribed by the seismic requirements of this

data.rule: Every structure and portion thereof, including nonstructural components, shall be designed and constructed to resist the effects of earthquake motions as prescribed by the seismic requirements of this standard. Certain nonbuilding structures, as described in Chapter 15, are also within the scope and shall be designed and constructed in accordance with the requirements of Chapter 15. The following structures are exempt from the seismic requirements of this standard:

title: Detached oneand two-family dwellings that are located where the mapped, short period, spectral response acceleration parameter, SS , is less than 0.4 or where the seismic design category determined in

data.rule: Detached oneand two-family dwellings that are located where the mapped, short period, spectral response acceleration parameter, SS , is less than 0.4 or where the seismic design category determined in accordance with Section 11.6 is A, B, or C.

title: Detached oneand two-family wood-frame dwellings not included in Exemption 1 with not more than two stories above grade plane, satisfying the limitations of and constructed in accordance with the IRC.

data.rule: Detached oneand two-family wood-frame dwellings not included in Exemption 1 with not more than two stories above grade plane, satisfying the limitations of and constructed in accordance with the IRC.

title: Agricultural storage structures that are intended only for incidental human occupancy.

data.rule: Agricultural storage structures that are intended only for incidental human occupancy.

title: Nonbuilding structures that require special consideration of their response characteristics and environment that are not addressed in Chapter 15 and for which other regulations provide seismic criteri

data.rule: Nonbuilding structures that require special consideration of their response characteristics and environment that are not addressed in Chapter 15 and for which other regulations provide seismic criteria, such as vehicular bridges, electrical transmission towers, hydraulic structures, buried utility lines and their appurtenances, and nuclear reactors.

title: Piers and wharves that are not accessible to the general public.

data.rule: Piers and wharves that are not accessible to the general public.

title: 11.1.3 Applicability

data.rule: 11.1.3 Applicability

title: Structures and their nonstructural components shall be designed and constructed in accordance with the requirements of the following chapters based on the type of structure or component:

data.rule: Structures and their nonstructural components shall be designed and constructed in accordance with the requirements of the following chapters based on the type of structure or component: Buildings: Chapter 12

title: Nonbuilding structures: Chapter 15

data.rule: Nonbuilding structures: Chapter 15

title: Nonstructural components: Chapter 13

data.rule: Nonstructural components: Chapter 13

title: Seismically isolated structures: Chapter 17

data.rule: Seismically isolated structures: Chapter 17

title: Structures with damping systems: Chapter 18

data.rule: Structures with damping systems: Chapter 18

title: 11.1.4 Alternate Materials and Methods of Construction

data.rule: 11.1.4 Alternate Materials and Methods of Construction

title: Alternate materials and methods of construction to those prescribed in the seismic requirements of this standard shall not be used unless approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction. Substantiating

data.rule: Alternate materials and methods of construction to those prescribed in the seismic requirements of this standard shall not be used unless approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction. Substantiating evidence shall be submitted demonstrating that the proposed alternate will be at least equal in strength, durability, and seismic resistance for the purpose intended.

title: 11.1.5 Quality Assurance

data.rule: 11.1.5 Quality Assurance

title: Quality assurance for seismic forceresisting systems and other designated seismic systems de fi ned in Section 13.2.2 shall be provided in accordance with the requirements of the Authority Having Juri

data.rule: Quality assurance for seismic forceresisting systems and other designated seismic systems de fi ned in Section 13.2.2 shall be provided in accordance with the requirements of the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

title: Where the Authority Having Jurisdiction has not adopted quality assurance requirements, or where the adopted requirements are not applicable to the seismic force-resisting system or designated seismic

data.rule: Where the Authority Having Jurisdiction has not adopted quality assurance requirements, or where the adopted requirements are not applicable to the seismic force-resisting system or designated seismic systems as described in Section 13.2.2, the registered design professional in responsible charge of designing the seismic force-resisting system or other designated seismic systems shall submit a quality assurance plan to the Authority Having Jurisdiction for approval. The quality assurance plan shall specify the quality assurance program elements to be implemented.
11.2 DEFINITIONS

title: The following de fi nitions apply only to the seismic provisions of Chapters 11 through 22 of this standard.

data.rule: The following de fi nitions apply only to the seismic provisions of Chapters 11 through 22 of this standard.

title: ACTIVE FAULT: A fault determined to be active by the Authority Having Jurisdiction from properly substantiated data (e.g., most recent mapping of active faults by the US Geological Survey).

data.definition: A fault determined to be active by the Authority Having Jurisdiction from properly substantiated data (e.g., most recent mapping of active faults by the US Geological Survey).

title: ADDITION: An increase in building area, aggregate fl oor area, height, or number of stories of a structure.

data.definition: An increase in building area, aggregate fl oor area, height, or number of stories of a structure.

title: ALTERATION: Any construction or renovation to an existing structure other than an addition.

data.definition: Any construction or renovation to an existing structure other than an addition.

title: APPENDAGE: An architectural component such as a canopy, marquee, ornamental balcony, or statuary.

data.definition: An architectural component such as a canopy, marquee, ornamental balcony, or statuary.

title: APPROVAL: The written acceptance by the Authority Having Jurisdiction of documentation that establishes the quali fi cation of a material, system, component, procedure, or person to ful fi ll the requ

data.definition: The written acceptance by the Authority Having Jurisdiction of documentation that establishes the quali fi cation of a material, system, component, procedure, or person to ful fi ll the requirements of this standard for the intended use.

title: ATTACHMENTS: Means by which nonstructural components or supports of nonstructural components are secured or connected to the seismic force-resisting system of the structure. Such attachments include a

data.definition: Means by which nonstructural components or supports of nonstructural components are secured or connected to the seismic force-resisting system of the structure. Such attachments include anchor bolts, welded connections, and mechanical fasteners.

title: BASE: The level at which the horizontal seismic ground motions are considered to be imparted to the structure.

data.definition: The level at which the horizontal seismic ground motions are considered to be imparted to the structure.

title: BASE SHEAR: Total design lateral force or shear at the base.

data.definition: Total design lateral force or shear at the base.

title: BOUNDARY ELEMENTS: Portions along wall and diaphragm edges for transferring or resisting forces. Boundary elements include chords and collectors at diaphragm and shear wall perimeters, edges of openin

data.definition: Portions along wall and diaphragm edges for transferring or resisting forces. Boundary elements include chords and collectors at diaphragm and shear wall perimeters, edges of openings, discontinuities, and reentrant corners.

title: BUILDING: Any structure whose intended use includes shelter of human occupants.

data.definition: Any structure whose intended use includes shelter of human occupants.

title: CANTILEVERED COLUMN SYSTEM: A seismic force-resisting system in which lateral forces are resisted entirely by columns acting as cantilevers from the base.

data.definition: A seismic force-resisting system in which lateral forces are resisted entirely by columns acting as cantilevers from the base.

title: CHARACTERISTIC EARTHQUAKE: An earthquake assessed for an active fault having a magnitude equal to the best estimate of the maximum magnitude capable of occurring on the fault but not less than the lar

data.definition: An earthquake assessed for an active fault having a magnitude equal to the best estimate of the maximum magnitude capable of occurring on the fault but not less than the largest magnitude that has occurred historically on the fault.

title: COLLECTOR (DRAG STRUT, TIE, DIAPHRAGM STRUT): A diaphragm or shear wall boundary element parallel to the applied load that collects and transfers diaphragm shear forces to the vertical elements of the

data.definition: A diaphragm or shear wall boundary element parallel to the applied load that collects and transfers diaphragm shear forces to the vertical elements of the seismic force-resisting system or distributes forces within the diaphragm or shear wall.

title: COMPONENT: A part of an architectural, electrical, or mechanical system.

data.definition: A part of an architectural, electrical, or mechanical system.

title: Component, Flexible: Nonstructural component that has a fundamental period greater than 0.06 s.

data.rule: Component, Flexible: Nonstructural component that has a fundamental period greater than 0.06 s.

title: Component, Nonstructural: A part of an architectural, mechanical, or electrical system within or without a building or nonbuilding structure.

data.rule: Component, Nonstructural: A part of an architectural, mechanical, or electrical system within or without a building or nonbuilding structure.

title: Component, Rigid: Nonstructural component that has a fundamental period less than or equal to 0.06 s.

data.rule: Component, Rigid: Nonstructural component that has a fundamental period less than or equal to 0.06 s.

title: Component, Rugged: A nonstructural component that has been shown to consistently function after design earthquake level or greater seismic events (based on past earthquake experience data or past seis

data.rule: Component, Rugged: A nonstructural component that has been shown to consistently function after design earthquake level or greater seismic events (based on past earthquake experience data or past seismic testing) when adequately anchored or supported. The classi fi cation of a nonstructural component as rugged shall be based on a comparison of the speci fi c component with components of similar strength and stiffness. Common examples of rugged components are AC motors, compressors, and base-mounted horizontal pumps.

title: CONCRETE:

data.rule: CONCRETE:

title: Plain Concrete: Concrete that is either unreinforced or contains less reinforcement than the minimum amount speci fi ed in ACI 318 for reinforced concrete.

data.rule: Plain Concrete: Concrete that is either unreinforced or contains less reinforcement than the minimum amount speci fi ed in ACI 318 for reinforced concrete.

title: Reinforced Concrete: Prestressed or nonprestressed concrete reinforced with no less reinforcement than the minimum amount required by ACI 318 and designed on the assumption that the two materials act

data.rule: Reinforced Concrete: Prestressed or nonprestressed concrete reinforced with no less reinforcement than the minimum amount required by ACI 318 and designed on the assumption that the two materials act together in resisting forces.

title: CONSTRUCTION DOCUMENTS: The written, graphic, electronic, and pictorial documents describing the design, locations, and physical characteristics of the project required to verify compliance with this

data.definition: The written, graphic, electronic, and pictorial documents describing the design, locations, and physical characteristics of the project required to verify compliance with this standard.

title: COUPLING BEAM: Abeam that is used to connect adjacent concrete wall elements to make them act together as a unit to resist lateral loads.

data.definition: Abeam that is used to connect adjacent concrete wall elements to make them act together as a unit to resist lateral loads.

title: DEFORMABILITY: The ratio of the ultimate deformation to the limit deformation.

data.definition: The ratio of the ultimate deformation to the limit deformation.

title: High-Deformability Element: An element whose deformability is not less than 3.5 where subjected to four fully reversed cycles at the limit deformation.

data.rule: High-Deformability Element: An element whose deformability is not less than 3.5 where subjected to four fully reversed cycles at the limit deformation.

title: Limited-Deformability Element: An element that is neither a low-deformability nor a high-deformability element.

data.rule: Limited-Deformability Element: An element that is neither a low-deformability nor a high-deformability element.

title: Low-Deformability Element: An element whose deformability is 1.5 or less.

data.rule: Low-Deformability Element: An element whose deformability is 1.5 or less.

title: DEFORMATION:

data.rule: DEFORMATION:

title: Limit Deformation: Two times the initial deformation that occurs at a load equal to 40% of the maximum strength.

data.rule: Limit Deformation: Two times the initial deformation that occurs at a load equal to 40% of the maximum strength.

title: Ultimate Deformation: The deformation at which failure occurs and that shall be deemed to occur if the sustainable load reduces to 80% or less of the maximum strength.

data.rule: Ultimate Deformation: The deformation at which failure occurs and that shall be deemed to occur if the sustainable load reduces to 80% or less of the maximum strength.

title: DESIGN EARTHQUAKE: The earthquake effects that are two-thirds of the corresponding risk-targeted maximum considered earthquake (MCER).

data.definition: The earthquake effects that are two-thirds of the corresponding risk-targeted maximum considered earthquake (MCER).

title: DESIGN EARTHQUAKE DISPLACEMENT: See DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT. See DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT. See DESIGN STORY DRIFT .

data.definition: See DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT. See DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT. See DESIGN STORY DRIFT .

title: DESIGN EARTHQUAKE GROUND MOTION: The earthquake ground motions that are two-thirds of the corresponding MCER ground motions.

data.definition: The earthquake ground motions that are two-thirds of the corresponding MCER ground motions.

title: DESIGNATED SEISMIC SYSTEMS: Those nonstructural components that require design in accordance with Chapter 13 and for which the component Importance Factor, I p , is greater than 1.0.

data.definition: Those nonstructural components that require design in accordance with Chapter 13 and for which the component Importance Factor, I p , is greater than 1.0.

title: DIAPHRAGM: Roof, fl oor, or other membrane or bracing system acting to transfer the lateral forces to the vertical resisting elements.

data.definition: Roof, fl oor, or other membrane or bracing system acting to transfer the lateral forces to the vertical resisting elements.

title: Flexure-Controlled Diaphragm: Diaphragm with a fl exural yielding mechanism, which limits the maximum forces that develop in the diaphragm, and a design shear strength or factored nominal shear capaci

data.rule: Flexure-Controlled Diaphragm: Diaphragm with a fl exural yielding mechanism, which limits the maximum forces that develop in the diaphragm, and a design shear strength or factored nominal shear capacity greater than the shear corresponding to the nominal fl exural strength.

title: Shear-Controlled Diaphragm: Diaphragm that does not meet the requirements of a fl exure-controlled diaphragm.

data.rule: Shear-Controlled Diaphragm: Diaphragm that does not meet the requirements of a fl exure-controlled diaphragm.

title: Transfer Forces, Diaphragm: Forces that occur in a diaphragm caused by transfer of seismic forces from the vertical seismic force-resisting elements above the diaphragm to other vertical seismic force

data.rule: Transfer Forces, Diaphragm: Forces that occur in a diaphragm caused by transfer of seismic forces from the vertical seismic force-resisting elements above the diaphragm to other vertical seismic force-resisting elements below the diaphragm because of offsets in the placement of the vertical elements or changes in relative lateral stiffnesses of the vertical elements.

title: Vertical Diaphragm : See WALL: Shear Wall.

data.rule: Vertical Diaphragm : See WALL: Shear Wall.

title: DIAPHRAGM BOUNDARY: A location where shear is transferred into or out of the diaphragm element. Transfer is either to a boundary element or to another force-resisting element.

data.definition: A location where shear is transferred into or out of the diaphragm element. Transfer is either to a boundary element or to another force-resisting element.

title: DIAPHRAGM CHORD: A diaphragm boundary element perpendicular to the applied load that is assumed to take axial stresses caused by the diaphragm moment.

data.definition: A diaphragm boundary element perpendicular to the applied load that is assumed to take axial stresses caused by the diaphragm moment.

title: DIAPHRAGM DEFORMATION: The relative horizontal displacement of portions of a diaphragm due to strain in the diaphragm elements and not due to deformations of the vertical elements of the seismic-force

data.definition: The relative horizontal displacement of portions of a diaphragm due to strain in the diaphragm elements and not due to deformations of the vertical elements of the seismic-force resisting system or to diaphragmrotation effects.

title: DIAPHRAGM-ROTATION EFFECTS: Relative horizontal displacement of portions of a diaphragm due to unequal deformations of the vertical elements of the seismic-force resisting system.

data.rule: DIAPHRAGM-ROTATION EFFECTS: Relative horizontal displacement of portions of a diaphragm due to unequal deformations of the vertical elements of the seismic-force resisting system.

title: DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT:

data.rule: DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT:

title: Design Earthquake Displacement: The displacement at a given location of the structure corresponding to the Design Earthquake.

data.rule: Design Earthquake Displacement: The displacement at a given location of the structure corresponding to the Design Earthquake.

title: Design Story Drift: The story drift corresponding to the Design Earthquake, taken at a representative plan location (center of mass or building perimeter, as required by Section 12.8.6).

data.rule: Design Story Drift: The story drift corresponding to the Design Earthquake, taken at a representative plan location (center of mass or building perimeter, as required by Section 12.8.6).

title: Maximum Considered Earthquake Displacement: The displacement at a given location of the structure corresponding to the Risk-Targeted Maximum Considered Earthquake (MCER).

data.rule: Maximum Considered Earthquake Displacement: The displacement at a given location of the structure corresponding to the Risk-Targeted Maximum Considered Earthquake (MCER).

title: Story Drift: The horizontal displacement at the top of the story relative to the bottom of the story at vertically aligned points corresponding to the given loading.

data.rule: Story Drift: The horizontal displacement at the top of the story relative to the bottom of the story at vertically aligned points corresponding to the given loading.

title: Story Drift Ratio: The story drift divided by the story height, hsx .

data.rule: Story Drift Ratio: The story drift divided by the story height, hsx .

title: DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM: An interconnected system composed primarily of linear components including piping, tubing, conduit, raceways, or ducts. Distribution systems include in-line components such as val

data.definition: An interconnected system composed primarily of linear components including piping, tubing, conduit, raceways, or ducts. Distribution systems include in-line components such as valves, in-line suspended pumps, and mixing boxes.

title: ELEMENT ACTION: Element axial, shear, or fl exural behavior.

data.definition: Element axial, shear, or fl exural behavior.

title: Critical Action: An action, failure of which would result in the collapse of multiple bays or multiple stories of the building or would result in a signi fi cant reduction in the structure ' s seismic

data.rule: Critical Action: An action, failure of which would result in the collapse of multiple bays or multiple stories of the building or would result in a signi fi cant reduction in the structure ' s seismic resistance.

title: Deformation-Controlled Action: Element actions for which reliable inelastic deformation capacity is achievable without critical strength decay.

data.rule: Deformation-Controlled Action: Element actions for which reliable inelastic deformation capacity is achievable without critical strength decay.

title: Force-Controlled Action: Any element actions modeled with linear properties and element actions not classi fi ed as deformation-controlled.

data.rule: Force-Controlled Action: Any element actions modeled with linear properties and element actions not classi fi ed as deformation-controlled.

title: Noncritical Actions: An action, failure of which would not result in either collapse or signi fi cant loss of the structure ' s seismic resistance.

data.rule: Noncritical Actions: An action, failure of which would not result in either collapse or signi fi cant loss of the structure ' s seismic resistance.

title: Ordinary Action: An action, failure of which would result in only local collapse, comprising not more than one bay in a single story, and would not result in a signi fi cant reduction of the structure

data.rule: Ordinary Action: An action, failure of which would result in only local collapse, comprising not more than one bay in a single story, and would not result in a signi fi cant reduction of the structure ' s seismic resistance.

title: ENCLOSURE: An interior space surrounded by walls.

data.definition: An interior space surrounded by walls.

title: EQUIPMENT SUPPORT: Those structural members or assemblies of members or manufactured elements, including braces, frames, legs, lugs, snuggers, hangers, or saddles, that transmit gravity loads and oper

data.definition: Those structural members or assemblies of members or manufactured elements, including braces, frames, legs, lugs, snuggers, hangers, or saddles, that transmit gravity loads and operating loads between the equipment and the structure.

title: Equipment Support Structures and Platforms: Assemblies of members or manufactured elements other than integral supports, including, but not limited to, moment frames, braced frames, skids, legs longer

data.rule: Equipment Support Structures and Platforms: Assemblies of members or manufactured elements other than integral supports, including, but not limited to, moment frames, braced frames, skids, legs longer than 24 in. (600 mm), or walls that support one or more nonstructural components or systems.

title: Distribution System Support: Members that provide vertical or lateral seismic resistance for distribution systems, including but not limited to, hangers, braces, pipe racks, and trapeze assemblies.

data.rule: Distribution System Support: Members that provide vertical or lateral seismic resistance for distribution systems, including but not limited to, hangers, braces, pipe racks, and trapeze assemblies.

title: Equipment Support, Integral: Assemblies of members or manufactured elements and their associated attachments and base plates that provide vertical or lateral support for nonstructural components, are

data.rule: Equipment Support, Integral: Assemblies of members or manufactured elements and their associated attachments and base plates that provide vertical or lateral support for nonstructural components, are directly connected to both the nonstructural component and the attachment to the structure or foundation, and where the nonstructural component acts as part of the lateral force resisting system of the equipment support. Integral equipment supports include but are not limited to legs less than or equal to 24 in. (600 mm) in length, lugs, skirts, and saddles.

title: FLEXIBLE CONNECTIONS: Those connections between equipment components that permit rotational and/or translational movement without degradation of performance. Examples include universal joints, bellows

data.definition: Those connections between equipment components that permit rotational and/or translational movement without degradation of performance. Examples include universal joints, bellows expansion joints, and fl exible metal hose.

title: FOUNDATION GEOTECHNICAL CAPACITY: The maximum pressure or strength design capacity of a foundation based on the supporting soil, rock, or controlled low-strength material.

data.definition: The maximum pressure or strength design capacity of a foundation based on the supporting soil, rock, or controlled low-strength material.

title: FOUNDATION STRUCTURAL CAPACITY: The design strength of foundations or foundation components as provided by adopted material standards and as altered by the requirements of this standard.

data.definition: The design strength of foundations or foundation components as provided by adopted material standards and as altered by the requirements of this standard.

title: FRAME:

data.rule: FRAME:

title: Braced Frame: An essentially vertical truss, or its equivalent, of the concentric or eccentric type that is provided in a building frame system or dual system to resist seismic forces.

data.rule: Braced Frame: An essentially vertical truss, or its equivalent, of the concentric or eccentric type that is provided in a building frame system or dual system to resist seismic forces.

title: Concentrically Braced Frame (CBF): A braced frame in which the members are subjected primarily to axial forces. CBFs are categorized as ordinary concentrically braced frames (OCBFs) or special concent

data.rule: Concentrically Braced Frame (CBF): A braced frame in which the members are subjected primarily to axial forces. CBFs are categorized as ordinary concentrically braced frames (OCBFs) or special concentrically braced frames (SCBFs).

title: Eccentrically Braced Frame (EBF): A diagonally braced frame in which at least one end of each brace frames into a beam a short distance from a beam-column or from another diagonal brace.

data.rule: Eccentrically Braced Frame (EBF): A diagonally braced frame in which at least one end of each brace frames into a beam a short distance from a beam-column or from another diagonal brace.

title: Moment Frame: A frame in which members and joints resist lateral forces by fl exure and along the axis of the members. Moment frames are categorized as intermediate moment frames (IMFs), ordinary mome

data.rule: Moment Frame: A frame in which members and joints resist lateral forces by fl exure and along the axis of the members. Moment frames are categorized as intermediate moment frames (IMFs), ordinary moment frames (OMFs), and special moment frames (SMFs).

title: STRUCTURAL SYSTEM:

data.rule: STRUCTURAL SYSTEM:

title: Building Frame System: A structural system with an essentially complete space frame providing support for vertical loads. Seismic force resistance is provided by shear walls or braced frames.

data.rule: Building Frame System: A structural system with an essentially complete space frame providing support for vertical loads. Seismic force resistance is provided by shear walls or braced frames.

title: Dual System: A structural system with an essentially complete space frame providing support for vertical loads. Seismic force resistance is provided by moment-resisting frames and shear walls or brace

data.rule: Dual System: A structural system with an essentially complete space frame providing support for vertical loads. Seismic force resistance is provided by moment-resisting frames and shear walls or braced frames as prescribed in Section 12.2.5.1.

title: Shear Wall -Frame Interactive System: A structural system that uses combinations of ordinary reinforced concrete shear walls and ordinary reinforced concrete moment frames designed to resist lateral f

data.rule: Shear Wall -Frame Interactive System: A structural system that uses combinations of ordinary reinforced concrete shear walls and ordinary reinforced concrete moment frames designed to resist lateral forces in proportion to their rigidities considering interaction between shear walls and frames on all levels.

title: Space Frame System: A 3-D structural system composed of interconnected members, other than bearing walls, that is capable of supporting vertical loads and, where designed for such an application, is c

data.rule: Space Frame System: A 3-D structural system composed of interconnected members, other than bearing walls, that is capable of supporting vertical loads and, where designed for such an application, is capable of providing resistance to seismic forces.

title: FRICTION CLIP: A device that relies on friction to resist applied loads in one or more directions to anchor a nonstructural component. Friction is provided mechanically and is not due to gravity loads

data.definition: A device that relies on friction to resist applied loads in one or more directions to anchor a nonstructural component. Friction is provided mechanically and is not due to gravity loads.

title: GLAZED CURTAIN WALL: A nonbearing wall that extends beyond the edges of building fl oor slabs and includes a glazing material installed in the curtain wall framing.

data.definition: A nonbearing wall that extends beyond the edges of building fl oor slabs and includes a glazing material installed in the curtain wall framing.

title: GLAZED STOREFRONT: A nonbearing wall that is installed between fl oor slabs, typically including entrances, and includes a glazing material installed in the storefront framing.

data.definition: A nonbearing wall that is installed between fl oor slabs, typically including entrances, and includes a glazing material installed in the storefront framing.

title: GRADEPLANE: Ahorizontal reference plane representing the average of fi nished ground level adjoining the structure at all exterior walls. Where the fi nished ground level slopes away from the exterior

data.definition: Ahorizontal reference plane representing the average of fi nished ground level adjoining the structure at all exterior walls. Where the fi nished ground level slopes away from the exterior walls, the grade plane is established by the lowest points within the area between the structure and the property line or, where the property line is more than 6 ft (1,829 mm) from the structure, between the structure and points 6 ft (1,829 mm) from the structure.

title: HEATING, VENTILATING, AIR-CONDITIONING, AND REFRIGERATION (HVACR): The equipment, distribution systems, and terminals, excluding interconnecting piping and ductwork that provide, either collectively o

data.rule: HEATING, VENTILATING, AIR-CONDITIONING, AND REFRIGERATION (HVACR): The equipment, distribution systems, and terminals, excluding interconnecting piping and ductwork that provide, either collectively or individually, the processes of heating, ventilating, air-conditioning, or refrigeration to a building or portion of a building.

title: INSPECTION, SPECIAL: The observation of the work by a special inspector to determine compliance with the approved construction documents and these standards in accordance with the quality assurance pl

data.definition: The observation of the work by a special inspector to determine compliance with the approved construction documents and these standards in accordance with the quality assurance plan.

title: Continuous Special Inspection: The full-time observation of the work by a special inspector who is present in the area where work is being performed.

data.rule: Continuous Special Inspection: The full-time observation of the work by a special inspector who is present in the area where work is being performed.

title: Periodic Special Inspection: The part-time or intermittent observation of the work by a special inspector who is present in the area where work has been or is being performed.

data.rule: Periodic Special Inspection: The part-time or intermittent observation of the work by a special inspector who is present in the area where work has been or is being performed.

title: INSPECTOR, SPECIAL: A person approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction to perform special inspection, and who shall be identi fi ed as the owner ' s inspector.

data.definition: A person approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction to perform special inspection, and who shall be identi fi ed as the owner ' s inspector.

title: INVERTED PENDULUM-TYPE STRUCTURES: Structures in which more than 50% of the structure ' s mass is concentrated at the top of a slender, cantilevered structure and in which the stability of the mass at

data.rule: INVERTED PENDULUM-TYPE STRUCTURES: Structures in which more than 50% of the structure ' s mass is concentrated at the top of a slender, cantilevered structure and in which the stability of the mass at the top of the structure relies on rotational restraint to the top of the cantilevered element.

title: JOINT: The geometric volume common to intersecting members.

data.definition: The geometric volume common to intersecting members.

title: LIGHT-FRAME CONSTRUCTION: A method of construction where the structural assemblies (e.g., walls, fl oors, ceilings, and roofs) are primarily formed by a system of repetitive wood or cold-formed steel

data.rule: LIGHT-FRAME CONSTRUCTION: A method of construction where the structural assemblies (e.g., walls, fl oors, ceilings, and roofs) are primarily formed by a system of repetitive wood or cold-formed steel framing members or subassemblies of these members (e.g., trusses).

title: LONGITUDINAL REINFORCEMENT RATIO: Area of longitudinal reinforcement divided by the cross-sectional area of the concrete.

data.definition: Area of longitudinal reinforcement divided by the cross-sectional area of the concrete.

title: MAXIMUM CONSIDERED EARTHQUAKE DISPLACEMENT: See DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT

data.definition: See DISPLACEMENT AND DRIFT

title: MAXIMUM CONSIDERED EARTHQUAKE (MCE) GROUND MOTION: The most severe earthquake effects considered by this standard, more speci fi cally de fi ned in the following two terms:

data.definition: The most severe earthquake effects considered by this standard, more speci fi cally de fi ned in the following two terms:

title: Maximum Considered Earthquake Geometric Mean ( MCEG ) Peak Ground Acceleration: The most severe earthquake effects considered by this standard determined for geometric mean peak ground acceleration an

data.rule: Maximum Considered Earthquake Geometric Mean ( MCEG ) Peak Ground Acceleration: The most severe earthquake effects considered by this standard determined for geometric mean peak ground acceleration and without adjustment for targeted risk. The MCEG peak ground acceleration adjusted for site effects (PGA M) is used in this standard for evaluation of liquefaction, lateral spreading, seismic settlements, and other soil-related issues. In this standard, general procedures for determining PGAM are provided in Section 11.8.3; site-speci fi c procedures are provided in Section 21.5.

title: Risk-Targeted Maximum Considered Earthquake ( MCER ) Ground Motion Response Acceleration: The most severe earthquake effects considered by this standard determined for the orientation that results in

data.rule: Risk-Targeted Maximum Considered Earthquake ( MCER ) Ground Motion Response Acceleration: The most severe earthquake effects considered by this standard determined for the orientation that results in the largest maximum response to horizontal ground motions and with adjustment for targeted risk. In this standard, general procedures for determining the MCE R ground motion values are provided in Section 11.4.4; sitespeci fi c procedures are provided in Sections 21.1 and 21.2.

title: 11.4.1 Near-Fault Sites

data.rule: 11.4.1 Near-Fault Sites

title: Sites satisfying either of the following conditions shall be classi fi ed as near fault:

data.rule: Sites satisfying either of the following conditions shall be classi fi ed as near fault:

title: 9.5 mi (15 km) or less from the surface projection of a known active fault capable of producing M w 7 or larger events, or

data.rule: 9.5 mi (15 km) or less from the surface projection of a known active fault capable of producing M w 7 or larger events, or

title: 6.25 mi (10 km) or less from the surface projection of a known active fault capable of producing events Mw6 or larger, but smaller than Mw7.

data.rule: 6.25 mi (10 km) or less from the surface projection of a known active fault capable of producing events Mw6 or larger, but smaller than Mw7.

title: EXCEPTIONS:

data.rule: EXCEPTIONS:

title: Faults with estimated slip rate less than 0.04 in. (1 mm) per year shall not be used to determine whether a site is a nearfault site.

data.rule: Faults with estimated slip rate less than 0.04 in. (1 mm) per year shall not be used to determine whether a site is a nearfault site.

title: The surface projection used in the determination of nearfault site classi fi cation shall not include portions of the fault at depths of 6.25 mi (10 km) or greater.

data.rule: The surface projection used in the determination of nearfault site classi fi cation shall not include portions of the fault at depths of 6.25 mi (10 km) or greater.

title: 11.4.2 Site Class

data.rule: 11.4.2 Site Class

title: The site shall be classi fi ed as Site Class A, B, BC, C, CD, D, DE, E, or F in accordance with Chapter 20.

data.rule: The site shall be classi fi ed as Site Class A, B, BC, C, CD, D, DE, E, or F in accordance with Chapter 20.

title: 11.4.2.1 Default Site Conditions

data.rule: 11.4.2.1 Default Site Conditions

title: Where the soil properties are not known in suf fi cient detail to determine the site class, risktargeted maximum considered earthquake (MCER) spectral response accelerations shall be based on the most

data.rule: Where the soil properties are not known in suf fi cient detail to determine the site class, risktargeted maximum considered earthquake (MCER) spectral response accelerations shall be based on the most critical spectral response acceleration at each period of Site Class C, Site Class CD, and Site Class D, unless the Authority Having Jurisdiction determines, based on geotechnical data, that Site Class DE, E, or F soils are present at the site.

data.rule: Access for Risk Category IV Where operational access to a Risk Category IV structure is required through an adjacent structure, the adjacent structure shall conform to the requirements for Risk Category IV structures. Where operational access is less than 10 ft (3.048 m) from an interior lot line or another structure on the same lot, protection from potential falling debris from adjacent structures shall be provided by the owner of the Risk Category IV structure.
11.6 SEISMIC DESIGN CATEGORY

title: Structures shall be assigned a seismic design category in accordance with this section.

data.rule: Structures shall be assigned a seismic design category in accordance with this section.

title: Risk Category I, II, or III structures located where the mapped spectral response acceleration parameter at 1-s period, S 1 , is greater than or equal to 0.75 shall be assigned to Seismic Design Categ

data.rule: Risk Category I, II, or III structures located where the mapped spectral response acceleration parameter at 1-s period, S 1 , is greater than or equal to 0.75 shall be assigned to Seismic Design Category E. Risk Category IV structures located where the mapped spectral response acceleration parameter at 1-s period, S 1 , is greater than or equal to 0.75 shall be assigned to Seismic Design Category F. All other structures shall be assigned to a seismic design category based on their risk category and the design spectral response acceleration parameters, SDS and SD 1 , determined in accordance with Section 11.4.4. Each building and structure shall be assigned to the more severe seismic design category in accordance with Table 11.6-1 or 11.6-2, irrespective of the fundamental period of vibration of the structure, T . The provisions in Chapter 19 shall not be used to modify the spectral response acceleration parameters for determining seismic design category.

title: Geotechnical Investigation Report Requirements for Seismic Design Categories D through F The geotechnical investigation report for a structure assigned to Seismic Design Category D, E, or F shall incl

data.rule: Geotechnical Investigation Report Requirements for Seismic Design Categories D through F The geotechnical investigation report for a structure assigned to Seismic Design Category D, E, or F shall include all of the following, as applicable:

title: The determination of dynamic seismic lateral earth pressures on basement and retaining walls caused by design earthquake ground motions.

data.rule: The determination of dynamic seismic lateral earth pressures on basement and retaining walls caused by design earthquake ground motions.

title: The potential for liquefaction, seismically-induced permanent ground displacement, and soil strength loss evaluated for site peak ground acceleration, earthquake magnitude, and source characteristics

data.rule: The potential for liquefaction, seismically-induced permanent ground displacement, and soil strength loss evaluated for site peak ground acceleration, earthquake magnitude, and source characteristics consistent with the MCEG peak ground acceleration. Peak ground acceleration shall be determined based on either (1) a site-speci fi c study taking into account soil ampli fi cation effects as speci fi ed in Section 11.4.7 or (2) the value of the MCE G peak ground acceleration parameter PGAM from the USGS Seismic Design Geodatabase for the applicable site class.

title: Assessment of potential consequences of liquefaction, seismically-induced permanent ground displacement, and soil strength loss, including, but not limited to, estimation of total and differential set

data.rule: Assessment of potential consequences of liquefaction, seismically-induced permanent ground displacement, and soil strength loss, including, but not limited to, estimation of total and differential settlement, lateral soil movement, lateral soil loads on foundations, reduction in foundation soil-bearing capacity and lateral soil reaction, soil downdrag and reduction in axial and lateral soil reaction for pile foundations, increases in soil lateral pressures on retaining walls, and fl otation of buried structures.

title: Discussion of mitigation measures such as, but not limited to, selection of appropriate foundation type and depths, selection of appropriate structural systems to accommodate anticipated displacements

data.rule: Discussion of mitigation measures such as, but not limited to, selection of appropriate foundation type and depths, selection of appropriate structural systems to accommodate anticipated displacements and forces, ground stabilization, or any combination of these measures and how they shall be considered in the design of the structure.

data.rule: If the option to incorporate the effects of vertical seismic ground motions is exercised in lieu of the requirements of Section 12.4.2.2, the requirements of this section are permitted to be used in the determination of the vertical design earthquake ground motions. The requirements of Section 11.9.2 shall only apply to structures in Seismic Design Categories C, D, E, and F located in the conterminous United States at or west of -105 degrees longitude, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, US Virgin Islands, Guam and Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa. For structures in Seismic Design Categories C, D, E, and F located in the conterminous United States east of -105 degrees longitude, the value of SaMv shall be taken as two-thirds of the value of SaM . The requirements of Section 11.9.3 shall apply to all structures in the Seismic Design Categories C, D, E, and F.
